---
title: "Policy"
---

Alongside my academic work, I have advised governments and international
organizations on foreign policy, national security, and program evaluation —
ten years across three continents and two conflict zones. I hold a TS/SCI
clearance with full-scope polygraph (not activated).

## Government & Policy Experience

**Director of Research & Evaluation** — Performance Systems Inc., Washington, DC · *2015–2017*
Designed randomized trials, surveys, and evaluations of U.S. government overseas
programs; trained 350+ military and civilian personnel on decision-making and
threat analysis in conflict zones.

**Analyst for Policy & Programs** — Management Systems International, Baghdad & Washington, DC · *2010–2013*
Supported a USAID project to rebuild the Iraqi civil service; built the statistical
cost-measurement tools underpinning the first U.S.–Iraq Memorandum of Understanding
on civil-service reform.

**Public Policy Consultant** — Australian Government, various departments · *2008–2010*
Built risk-assessment frameworks and large-scale workforce analytics across 60
public-sector departments.

**Junior Professional Officer** — United Nations, Amman, Jordan · *2006–2007*
Developed indicators and surveys that informed an overhaul of education programs
for 400 schools and 40,000+ refugee students across four countries.

## Education

**Ph.D., Political Science** — The George Washington University · *2019*

**M.A., Strategic Studies** — The Australian National University · *2010*

**B.A., International Studies & Economics** — The University of New South Wales · *2007*
